data.title: Embraer ends Q1 with record $32.1B backlog, up 22% Y/Y
data.description:
Embraer (EMBJ) down 1.2% in Tuesday's trading, despite disclosing its firm order ​backlog rose 22% Y/Y to a ⁠record $32.1B ​at the end of Q1, marking the Brazilian planemaker’s sixth consecutive quarterly record.

The biggest boost came from the commercial aviation unit, where backlog jumped 50% Y/Y to $15B that was largely tied to 18 firm orders for the Embraer E195-E2 from Finnair, part of a broader agreement covering up to 46 aircraft, including options and purchase rights.

Services and support continued to expand, with backlog rising 11% to a record-high $5.1B, as Embraer (EMBJ) signed long-term agreements with Airnorth, the Hungarian Air Force, and Virgin Australia.

Backlog in Embraer's (EMBJ) executive aircraft business stayed flat at $7.6B both Y/Y and Q/Q, even as deliveries increased to 29 aircraft from 23 a year earlier.

In defense, the company's backlog reached $4.4B, up 5% Y/Y but down 4% Q/Q.

The company said it delivered 44 aircraft across all business units in Q1, a 47% increase compared with 30 deliveries in the year-earlier quarter.

data.title: BlackRock, StanChart, crypto trading ​platform launch joint framework
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK), Standard Chartered (SCBFF) (SCBFY), and the crypto trading ​platform OKX have launched a joint framework to establish new utility for tokenized real-world assets.

BlackRock's BUIDL tokenized short-term treasury fund will be integrated into collateral workflows, with StanChart set to act as a custodian in such an arrangement.

The BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund is a tokenized money market fund that provides qualified institutional investors with USD yields via blockchain.

Select OKX clients can post the treasury fund as collateral held off-exchange in regulated custody at the globally systemically important bank, while trading on OKX Middle East, eliminating the need to move assets between venues.

Furthermore, BUIDL can be deposited and traded on-exchange, and used as yield-bearing collateral for margin trading.

data.title: Oil leads, bonds bleed: Deutsche Bank tracks markets two months after Iran conflict began
data.description:
Two months after the attack on Iran began, Deutsche Bank Research has assessed how a sample of financial assets has responded, and while much of the market action follows the standard oil shock playbook, the bank flags several surprising divergences worth noting.

Oil is leading the way, as Deutsche Bank expected. Brent 1-month contracts are up 49% since the conflict began, though the bank notes investors still appear to be treating the disruption as temporary, the 6-month Brent contract is up a comparatively modest 25%, suggesting markets are not pricing in a prolonged supply squeeze.

US assets have outperformed the rest of the world, which Deutsche Bank attributes to America's insulation as a net energy exporter, amplified by a sharp rebound in tech stocks through April that pushed both the S&P 500 and NASDAQ to fresh record highs yesterday.

South Korea's KOSPI has also been a standout, with Deutsche Bank pointing out that its YTD total return now stands above 58% in local currency terms since the conflict began.

The Eurozone tells the opposite story. Deutsche Bank highlights the region's greater exposure to the energy shock as the key drag, with the pain compounded further on a USD basis by currency moves, and all of this despite markets already pricing in at least two ECB rate hikes this year.

One of the more counterintuitive findings in Deutsche Bank's analysis is the weakness in precious metals.

Gold and silver are both down at least 10% since the conflict started, an unusual outcome during a geopolitical shock. The bank explains this through two lenses: both metals were coming off record highs when hostilities began, and since they typically move inversely to real rates, the sharp rise in yields has dampened demand.

On bonds, Deutsche Bank Research notes the damage has been severe across developed markets. 10-year yields have risen 74 basis points in the UK, 47bps in France, 40bps in the US, 43bps in Germany, and 35bps in Japan.

The bank attributes this partly to inflation consequences stemming from the oil shock, but also to mounting concerns over fiscal easing. Yields were already low coming into the conflict, shaped by earlier fears around AI-driven deflation and job displacement, concerns that Deutsche Bank notes now feel like a distant memory.

data.title: Cullinan wins FDA review for cancer therapy developed with Taiho
data.description:
• Cullinan Therapeutics (CGEM) announced on Tuesday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) has accepted a marketing application for zipalertinib, a cancer therapy developed by the company with Japanese drugmaker Taiho Pharmaceutical.
  • The New Drug Application seeks U.S. approval for the twice-daily oral therapy as a late-line option for patients with locally advanced or metastatic non-small cell lung cancer whose tumors harbor epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) exon 20 insertion (ex20ins) mutations.
  • The FDA has assigned February 27, 2027, as the target action date for the NDA, Cullinan (CGEM), and Taiho announced in a statement.
  • The NDA is supported by data from the companies’ Phase 2b portion of the REZILIENT1 Phase 1/2 clinical trial, in which Ezipalertinib as a late-line therapy met the primary endpoint of objective response rate in NSCLC patients harboring EGFR ex20ins mutations.

data.title: Herc Holdings surges following Q1 results beat
data.description:
Herc Holdings (HRI) shares surged about 10% following the company’s stronger-than-expected first-quarter results, as investors responded positively to solid revenue growth and resilient demand trends despite earnings pressure.

The equipment rental provider reported non-GAAP EPS of $0.21, comfortably beating expectations, while revenue climbed 32.4% year over year to $1.14B, driven largely by a 33% jump in equipment rental revenue. Growth was fueled by a larger fleet following the H&E acquisition and continued strength in large-scale construction projects.

Adjusted EBITDA rose 33% to $448M, with margins holding steady at 39%, signaling operational discipline even as integration costs weighed on profitability. 

However, net losses widened to $24M due to higher depreciation and interest expenses tied to acquisition-related debt. 

Management reaffirmed its full-year 2026 outlook, projecting equipment rental revenue of $4.275B to $4.4B and adjusted EBITDA of $2B to $2.1B. The company expects performance to strengthen in the second half of the year as synergies from the acquisition ramp up.

data.title: Omnicell lifts 2026 non-GAAP EPS outlook to $1.80-$2.00 as Titan XT shipments are planned for 2H 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Omnicell (OMCL) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "We started 2026 with solid execution in the first quarter, delivering results at the high end or above our previously issued Q1 2026 guidance ranges across all key metrics" (Founder, Executive Chairman, President & CEO Randall Lipps), adding, "We continue to see constructive demand environment, including meaningful competitive conversion opportunities."
  • "Total revenue for the quarter was $310 million. Non-GAAP EBITDA was $45 million and non-GAAP earnings per share was $0.55" (President & CEO Lipps).
  • "For those new to the Omnicell story... we formally introduce Omnicell Titan XT" (President & CEO Lipps), and "We anticipate modest incremental Titan XT revenue in 2026. With initial hardware shipments planned to begin in the second half of the year, followed by a phased rollout of OmniSphere functionality in the first half of 2027" (President & CEO Lipps).
  • "For the full year 2026, we are maintaining our previously provided guidance for product bookings, ARR and total revenue, while increasing our guidance ranges for non-GAAP EBITDA and non-GAAP earnings per share" (Executive VP & CFO Baird Radford).

Outlook

  • "For the second quarter of 2026, we expect total revenue to be in the range of $307 million to $313 million" (Executive VP & CFO Radford), with "non-GAAP earnings per share to be in the range of $0.40 to $0.48" (Executive VP & CFO Radford).
  • "For full year 2026... product bookings in the range of $510 million to $560 million" (Executive VP & CFO Radford), "Total revenues... $1.215 billion to $1.255 billion" (Executive VP & CFO Radford), and "year-end 2026 annual recurring revenue, or ARR, is expected to be between $680 million to $700 million" (Executive VP & CFO Radford).
  • "Non-GAAP EBITDA is now expected to be between $153 million and $168 million compared to previous guidance of $145 million to $160 million" (Executive VP & CFO Radford), and "Non-GAAP earnings per share are now expected to be between $1.80 and $2 compared to $1.65 to $1.85 previously" (Executive VP & CFO Radford).

Financial Results

  • "Total revenue for the first quarter was $310 million, representing 15% year-over-year growth" (Executive VP & CFO Radford), driven by "steady execution within our points of care connected devices revenues as well as continued growth in our recurring revenue streams" (Executive VP & CFO Radford).
  • "Product revenue was $175 million, up 20% year-over-year" (Executive VP & CFO Radford) and "Service revenue was $135 million, increasing 8% year-over-year" (Executive VP & CFO Radford).
  • "For the first quarter of 2026, non-GAAP gross margin was approximately 46%" (Executive VP & CFO Radford), and "Free cash flow was $39 million in the first quarter of 2026" (Executive VP & CFO Radford).

Q&A

  • Stanislav Berenshteyn, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC: "I wanted to get an update on the retail segment... EnlivenHealth"; Executive VP & COO Nnamdi Njoku: "it's been a challenging time in the retail segment... that challenging sort of dynamic continues to play out," while "the volumes there continue to increase" and the focus is "how to deliver on those growing demand at the lowest cost."
  • Jessica Tassan, Piper Sandler & Co.: "articulating the sources of gross margin upside"; President & CEO Lipps: "we saw favorability in the product and customer mix" and "we lapped the investments... in field-based software upgrades," but "margins will continue to fluctuate from period to period."
  • Allen Lutz, BofA Securities: "XTExtend versus Titan XT" and "any increase in cancellations"; President & CEO Lipps: "probably less people are less interested in the XTExtend package and more interested in how to deploy and when to deploy the Titan XT" and customers are "reevaluating their configurations and their strategies to acquire the equipment."
  • Scott Schoenhaus, KeyBanc Capital Markets Inc.: "what's embedded in your bookings guidance on the competitive conversion side"; President & CEO Lipps: "we've been taking share over an extended period of time" and "you see is a modest increase year-over-year and competitive wins assumed in our guidance."
  • Eugene Mannheimer, Prime Executions, Inc.: investments shifting; Executive VP & CFO Radford: "we had some places where we had the flexibility... to shift a little bit of money out into Q2 and Q3" and "I would expect to see some operating expense increases as we move into those periods."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts: slightly positive, with validation-focused follow-ups on durability of margins, bookings mix (XTExtend vs. Titan XT), competitive conversion assumptions, and retail/EnlivenHealth headwinds.
  • Management: slightly positive in prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing confidence and pacing caveats, including "We anticipate modest incremental Titan XT revenue in 2026" (President & CEO Lipps) and "margins will continue to fluctuate" (President & CEO Lipps).
  • Versus last quarter: management shifted from early Titan XT introduction messaging to execution and initial orders, including "During the first quarter of 2026, we booked our initial Titan XT orders" (Executive VP & CFO Radford), while analysts continued pressing on cycle timing and competitive conversion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from Q4’s initial 2026 outlook to a Q1 update that "increasing our guidance ranges for non-GAAP EBITDA and non-GAAP earnings per share" while "maintaining" bookings, ARR, and revenue (Executive VP & CFO Radford).
  • The tariff view changed from "approximately $15 million" in 2026 (Q4, Executive VP & CFO Radford) to "approximately $12 million" (Q1, Executive VP & CFO Radford), alongside a change in the assumed non-GAAP tax rate from "approximately 13%" (Q4, Executive VP & CFO Radford) to "approximately 15%" (Q1, Executive VP & CFO Radford).
  • Titan XT commercialization moved from Q4’s "available for shipment in the second half of 2026" framing to Q1’s confirmation that hardware shipments are "planned to begin in the second half of the year" with "initial Titan XT orders" already booked (President & CEO Lipps; Executive VP & CFO Radford).

Risks and Concerns

  • "We remain mindful of evolving macro environment uncertainty and capital spending dynamics" (Founder, Executive Chairman, President & CEO Lipps).
  • "Tariffs remain fluid and we continue to closely monitor" (Executive VP & CFO Radford), and 2026 guidance includes "approximately $12 million of tariff-related costs impacting the P&L" (Executive VP & CFO Radford).
  • "Health Systems capital approval cycles remain multi-quarter to multiyear activities" (Founder, Executive Chairman, President & CEO Lipps), contributing to "product bookings... weighted toward the back half of this year" (Executive VP & CFO Radford).

Final Takeaway

Omnicell management framed Q1 as a high-end start to 2026 with profitability upside and reiterated that Titan XT and OmniSphere are driving longer-term platform planning, while near-term pacing remains tied to multiquarter capital cycles. The company raised full-year non-GAAP EBITDA and EPS ranges, incorporated an updated tariff cost estimate, and positioned competitive conversions, recurring revenue expansion, and the Titan XT transition timeline—hardware shipments planned for 2H 2026 and OmniSphere functionality rollout in 1H 2027—as central to 2026 execution.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Super Micro bets on arm architecture as it expands data center offering
data.description:
• Super Micro Computer (SMCI) on Tuesday said it is expanding its data center portfolio with Arm-based servers amid surging infrastructure requirements of AI workloads.
  • The Arm-based processors used in the new system give higher performance per watt.
  • "With high-density, liquid-cooled systems and energy-efficient Arm architectures, we enable scalable, flexible data centers that maximize performance-per-watt and accelerate AI adoption across cloud and enterprise environments," Supermicro CEO said.
  • Super Micro says customers can assemble data centers modularly, starting from components (servers, GPUs, networking) up to full racks, using standardized designs.
  • The company also launched new servers with more processing power and memory for cloud and enterprise use.
  • Shares down -3%

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: KO, GLW, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were cautious on Tuesday as investors weighed reports that President Donald Trump was not satisfied with Iran’s latest proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and defer nuclear talks.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • The Coca-Cola Company (KO) +6% - Shares advanced after delivering a Q1 beat, with revenue up 12.6% Y/Y to $12.5B and organic sales growth of 10% topping estimates, driven by strength across regions and higher concentrate sales. Comparable EPS climbed 18% Y/Y to $0.86, also above consensus, while the company maintained its full-year outlook for ~4%–5% organic revenue growth and 7%–8% EPS growth.
  • Centene Corporation (CNC) +6% - Shares rose after reporting better-than-expected Q1 results driven by a lower health benefits ratio and raising its full-year outlook, now guiding for revenue of $187.5B–$191.5B and adjusted EPS of more than $3.40, up from prior guidance of over $3.00 on $186.5B–$190.5B, and above analyst expectations of $188.8B in revenue and EPS above $3.02.

Biggest stock losers

  • Celestica (CLS) -14% - Shares dipped despite posting a strong Q1 beat and raising guidance, as investor expectations appeared even higher following the stock’s prior run. The company delivered robust 52.8% Y/Y revenue growth and topped EPS estimates while also lifting its 2026 outlook and issuing upbeat Q2 guidance. However, the selloff suggests profit-taking and concerns that much of the strong growth and AI-driven momentum were already priced in.
  • Spotify Technology S.A. (SPOT) -11% - Shares plunged as weaker-than-expected Q2 premium subscriber guidance (299M vs. 300.3M consensus) overshadowed a strong Q1 beat, where revenue rose 8%, profits nearly tripled to €3.45, and free cash flow reached a record €824M with margin expansion. For Q2, the company guided to higher total revenue, gross margin, and MAUs above expectations, but the softer subscriber outlook weighed on sentiment.
  • Corning (GLW) -7% - Shares dipped after guiding Q2 sales to ~$4.6B, below the $4.67B consensus, overshadowing a Q1 beat where core sales rose 18% Y/Y to ~$4.35B and core EPS climbed ~30% Y/Y to $0.70, driven by strong Optical Communications growth (+36% Y/Y). The outlook also includes an extended solar wafer facility shutdown, adding ~$30M in costs, with Q2 EPS seen at $0.73–$0.77.
  • NOV (NOV) -3% - Shares fell after Q1 results showed a GAAP EPS miss at $0.05, while revenue of $2.05B (-2.4% Y/Y) came in line with estimates. The company returned $100M to shareholders through buybacks and dividends but guided Q2 revenue to decline 4%–6% Y/Y with adjusted EBITDA of $185M–$215M, citing ongoing uncertainty and disruptions tied to the Middle East conflict that impacted operations, logistics, and costs during the quarter.

data.title: Curbline targets $850M of 2026 investments and lifts OFFO guidance to $1.20-$1.23
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Curbline Properties Corp. (CURB) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "We had an incredibly productive and active start to the year as investment opportunities have remained elevated, leasing demand has remained strong, and we've tapped new markets, increasing our liquidity and access to capital" (President, CEO & Director David Lukes).
  • "This activity is falling directly to the bottom line, leading to an increase in our OFFO guidance range" (President, CEO & Director Lukes).
  • "This elevated level of activity has continued putting us in a position to raise our 2026 investment target to $850 million from $750 million" (President, CEO & Director Lukes).
  • "We've signed over 145,000 square feet of new leases and renewals this quarter" (President, CEO & Director Lukes).
  • "In terms of same-property growth, we generated almost 5% growth in the quarter, and our capital expenditures were just 6.3% of quarterly NOI" (President, CEO & Director Lukes).
  • "First quarter results were ahead of budget, largely due to higher NOI... along with lower G&A expenses" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Conor Fennerty).
  • "We are increasing OFFO guidance to a range between $1.20 and $1.23 per share" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).

Outlook

  • Management did not provide revenue or EPS guidance in the transcript, and the analysts’ estimates data provided in the prompt is not in a valid quarter format for the required comparison.
  • "We are increasing OFFO guidance to a range between $1.20 and $1.23 per share" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).
  • "Underpinning the midpoint of the range is: One, roughly $850 million of full year investments... three, CapEx as a percentage of NOI of less than 10%; and four, G&A of roughly $32 million" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).
  • "We continue to forecast [same-property NOI] growth of 3% at the midpoint in 2026" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty), while adding that "in the second quarter... a difficult uncollectible revenue comparison will act as an almost 300 basis point headwind" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).

Financial Results

  • Management did not disclose total revenue or EPS in the prepared remarks.
  • "NOI was up 3% sequentially and over 50% year-over-year" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).
  • "Same-property NOI was up 4.8% for the first quarter" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).
  • "The lease rate was up 30 basis points year-over-year to 96.3%, with occupancy up 60 basis points" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).
  • "In the first quarter and the second quarter to date, the company sold 11.8 million shares on a forward basis with $296 million of expected gross proceeds" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty), and "the company ended the quarter with a leverage ratio of approximately 20%" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).

Q&A

  • Ronald Kamdem: "Is there any sort of larger deals in that pipeline?"; President, CEO & Director Lukes: "the pipeline at this point is exclusively individual properties. There's no portfolios of note."; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty: "we're in the low 6s" and "an unlevered IRR in the 7% to 9%."
  • Ronald Kamdem: long-term same-store growth; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty: "this is a 2.5% to 4% business" and "we're probably in the high end of that range."
  • Craig Mailman, Citi: consumer/foot traffic sensitivity; President, CEO & Director Lukes: "It's not a great proxy that we use to find out tenant profitability" and "I think that the insulation for us is probably more to do with consumer behavior and a little bit less so on the economy."
  • Floris Van Dijkum, Ladenburg: GAAP vs cash cap rates; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty: "our average differential between GAAP and cash... is about 35 basis points" and "All the numbers that we've referenced have always been cash."
  • Todd Thomas, KeyBanc: seller cohort and pipeline visibility; President, CEO & Director Lukes: "we have definitely started to shift our deal teams to not only be building relationships with brokers, but also estate planning attorneys"; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty: "we have closed, we have under contract or have been awarded about 90% of that $850 million bogey."
  • Paulina Rojas Schmidt, Green Street: Whitestone read-through and macro risk; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty: "there are probably more differences than similarities"; Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty: "our balance sheet... affords us a lot of cushion"; President, CEO & Director Lukes: "risk is very much correlated to the size of the bet... And this business is so granular."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were neutral-to-slightly negative on macro and risk, using prompts like "markets have been volatile" and "risk off attitude" (Paulina Rojas Schmidt, Green Street) and probing tenant sensitivity (Craig Mailman, Citi).
  • Management remained slightly positive and process-focused, emphasizing durability and balance sheet positioning, including "we are continuing to tailor our team" (President, CEO & Director Lukes) and "affords us a lot of cushion" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).
  • Versus last quarter’s more reflective tone ("The fourth quarter capped an incredible first year as a public company" (President, CEO & Director Lukes)), the current call leaned more execution-forward with repeated emphasis on raised targets and visibility ("raise our 2026 investment target" (President, CEO & Director Lukes); "about 90%" visibility (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty)).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance and targets moved higher: Q4 introduced "FFO guidance... between $1.17 and $1.21" with "roughly $700 million of full year investments" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty), while Q1 raised to "OFFO guidance... between $1.20 and $1.23" and "$850 million" of investments (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty; President, CEO & Director Lukes).
  • Pipeline visibility tightened: Q4 said visibility was "about half" of the pipeline (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty), while Q1 said "about 90%" of the $850 million is closed/under contract/awarded (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).
  • Capital markets activity expanded: Q4 noted "5.2 million shares" forward for "$120 million" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty), while Q1 cited "11.8 million shares" forward for "$296 million" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).

Risks and Concerns

  • "In the second quarter... a difficult uncollectible revenue comparison will act as an almost 300 basis point headwind" to same-property NOI growth (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty), with mitigation framed as back-half acceleration: "second half base rent growth expected to average over 4%" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).
  • Acquisition execution risk remains tied to diligence timing: "that pipeline has some risk to it until we're through due diligence" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).
  • Potential dilution from forward settlements: "dilution from the forward offerings [is]... an approximately $0.01 per share headwind to 2026 OFFO" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Fennerty).

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that elevated acquisition opportunities and leasing demand led Curbline to raise its 2026 investment target to $850 million and lift OFFO guidance to $1.20-$1.23 per share, supported by what the CFO described as roughly 90% visibility on the investment bogey. Executives also highlighted capital efficiency (CapEx below 10% of NOI in guidance), a low-6% cash cap-rate environment with 7%-9% unlevered IRR targets, and a balance sheet positioned with substantial liquidity and ~20% leverage, while flagging a near-term Q2 headwind to same-property NOI growth tied to uncollectible revenue comparisons and the timing of recoverable CapEx.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Zimmer Biomet falls after CEO exit, unchanged growth outlook
data.description:
Zimmer Biomet (ZBH) lost ~6%, potentially on track to record its biggest single-day decline since November on Tuesday after the orthopedic device maker announced the departure of its CFO and maintained its full-year revenue outlook with its Q1 2026 results.

Suketu Upadhyay, who served Zimmer (ZBH) as CFO since 2019, will depart effective April 28 for a new career opportunity, with Chief Accounting Officer Paul Stellato taking over on an interim basis until a permanent replacement is found, the company said.

Stellato, who joined Zimmer (ZBH) in 2022, has helped the company implement its capital allocation strategy, including its recent acquisitions and share repurchase program, the Warsaw, Indiana-based MedTech noted.

"We are deeply grateful for Suky's leadership and contributions to Zimmer Biomet over the past seven years," added CEO Ivan Tornos.

As for Q1 financials, Zimmer (ZBH) reported $2.09 of adjusted diluted earnings per share on $2.1B in sales with ~16% YoY and ~9% YoY growth, exceeding the consensus by $20M and $0.23, respectively.

ZBH’s Hips net sales for the quarter reached $524M with a ~6% YoY growth, beating the consensus of $517.7M according to Bloomberg data, while Knees net sales jumped ~5% YoY to $828.6M, missing $832.3M projected by analysts.

Looking ahead, the company estimates 2.5% YoY - 4.5% YoY revenue growth for 2026, unchanged from its prior forecast and ~3.9% YoY growth in the consensus. However, Zimmer (ZBH) lifted its adjusted EPS outlook to $8.40 - $8.55 from $8.30 - $8.45 in the previous guidance, in line with the average analyst estimate of $8.40.
